note:
随着Epic Games和苹果之间的法律战即将于9月28日举行听证会，这家苹果制造商直言不讳地驳斥了史诗电影要求初步禁令的请求，这将把堡垒之夜带回iOS应用商店。在周二晚间提交的一份长达37页的动议中，苹果表示，它需要保留惩罚这是苹果与任何开发者经历过的最恶劣的破坏行为之一的能力。
Etic引发了一场大火，并向其倒了汽油，现在又请求法院紧急协助扑灭它，尽管Etic自己只需简单地遵守多年来一直有利可图地支配其与苹果公司关系的合同条款就可以立即做到这一点。动议中写道，在一定程度上，Etic自己就可以做到这一点，这些合同条款多年来一直有利可图地支配着它与苹果的关系。史诗是破坏者，不是烈士。
苹果不做任何改变就不让堡垒之夜进入苹果应用商店的理由，在很大程度上是基于保护苹果手机安全的理由。苹果最初创建iOS应用商店时表示，苹果没有重建互联网，而是选择为iPhone用户创建一个安全可信的地方，让他们发现和下载应用程序，因为他们相信，只需轻触手指，他们就能无缝可靠地工作。
EPIC违反了开发者协议的合同条款，并通过修补程序将自己的直接支付选项插入堡垒之夜的iOS版本，从而破坏了这种安全方式。苹果在堡垒之夜上线前无法对其进行审查。正因为如此，苹果表示，它无法确保Etic没有像往常那样改变支付系统，或者绕过iPhone的安全功能或隐私保护。
例如，目前的情况是，堡垒之夜的直接支付选项(之前在iOS上下载堡垒之夜的玩家仍然可以使用，并被广泛使用)并没有被iPhone内置的应用内购买家长控制限制所屏蔽。苹果公司写道，苹果向客户承诺，App Store将成为客户发现和下载应用程序的安全和值得信赖的地方。通过将秘密和未经审查的功能插入到应用程序中，Etic威胁到了苹果和iPhone客户之间的关系。
苹果警告说，如果EpicePic在这方面的论据取得成功，那么没有什么能阻止其他开发者也破坏苹果在应用内购买中30%的分成。苹果写道，Etic坚称，其他开发者不会效仿它，因为他们会担心遭到报复。但是没有人会害怕苹果公司的回应，如果法院批准了Etic寻求的禁令，并宣布所有开发者都可以藐视苹果公司的规则，只要他们声称苹果的规则是反竞争的，就不会有任何后果。
撇开堡垒之夜不谈，苹果还辩称，它必须保留关闭EpiceiOS开发者所有账户的权利。这包括iOS上虚幻引擎开发背后的账户，这些账户目前受到限制令的保护。
苹果公司(Apple)表示，它希望关闭Etic的所有账户，部分原因是为了防止它所称的应用程序从一个开发者账户转移到另一个账户的空壳游戏。但苹果公司表示，这也是因为该公司现在将Etic的虚幻引擎视为一个潜在的威胁，它构成了第二个潜在的特洛伊木马程序，使Etic能够继续其威胁，破坏App Store，并插入更多未经授权的功能。
苹果公司辩称，通过虚幻引擎，Etic可以将恶意软件或其他未经授权的功能(如替代的直接支付机制)插入到App Store上提供的、依赖虚幻引擎的非Etic应用程序中。很容易看出，一个影响世界上很大一部分iPhone操作的流氓应用程序可能会严重扰乱当地甚至世界范围内的电话系统，以及互联网本身的广泛领域。
尽管那些声称通过游戏引擎在全球范围内进行互联网破坏的说法可能有些牵强，但苹果的主要观点是，在iPhone安全和iOS开发合同义务方面，苹果不能再对Epic.。苹果公司写道，不会等到第二次被愚弄，才会因为其负责人的不良行为而终止附属公司。
苹果动议的其余部分在很大程度上重申了之前的论点，即Etic不太可能证明其关于苹果是对市场施加不合理控制的垄断者的说法。苹果写道，从游戏机、个人电脑到安卓手机，EpicePic享有分销堡垒之夜的另一种方式，这一事实提供了一个教科书般的例子，说明如果将这些服务用于同样的目的，这些服务可以合理地互换。
更重要的是，即便是在iOS上，苹果也表示，EpicePic还有其他选择可以让堡垒之夜货币化，而无需支付苹果30%的应用内购买手续费。该公司写道，开发者可以通过广告、实体商品和服务的销售，以及任何其他不会给苹果公司佣金的方式来创造收入，并补充说，App Store上超过80%的应用程序都使用这种商业模式。
苹果还重申了其立场，此前得到了法院的支持，即史诗在这件事上的伤害是自己造成的，因此不需要初步禁令来纠正。对于Etic声称声誉受损的说法，苹果在被迫下架App Store时指出，Etic围绕其违反与苹果协议的决定，进行了一场事先计划好的全面媒体闪电战，围绕这一努力发起了广告攻势，并一直持续到今天。如果Etic真的担心它会因为这场争端而名誉受损，它就不会参与这些精心策划的宣传活动。
title: 苹果在新的法庭文件中要求阻止iOS虚幻引擎开发
title_en: Apple asks to block iOS Unreal Engine development in new court filing

note:
美国国家海洋和大气管理局最近聘请了一名新人担任上级副助理部长一职。通常情况下，这不会太令人惊讶，也不会有太大的新闻价值，但这是一个例外。特拉华大学教授大卫·莱格茨(David Legates)加入美国国家海洋和大气局(NOAA)，担任“负责环境观测和预测的商务部副助理部长”，他是一位著名的逆向投资者，他拒绝人类造成气候变化的科学。
该职位显然向NOAA代理负责人尼尔·雅各布斯(Neil Jacobs)汇报，尽管招聘情况尚不清楚。ARS询问NOAA关于这一职位的职责，但该机构没有回应。雅各布斯卷入了特朗普总统关于飓风“多里安”的不准确推文的余波，最终导致一张用黑色记号笔篡改的预报地图达到顶峰。两项调查发现，雅各布斯屈服于商务部长威尔伯·罗斯(Wilbur Ross)办公室和白宫的指令，发布了一份未签署的NOAA声明，试图通过温和告诫纠正他的预测者来挽救总统不准确的声明。
据“华盛顿邮报”报道，特拉华大学莱格茨系在一封电子邮件中通知学生，他将不会在秋季学期任教，同时指出，“大卫希望春天能回到密歇根大学。”
莱格茨在2005年至2011年间是特拉华州的气候学家。虽然他的职业生涯始于研究降水数据和模式，但他主要以拒绝人类在气候变化中的作用而闻名。他经常为心脏地带研究所--一个反对气候科学事实的“智囊团”--工作。2015年，当Ars访问心脏地带会议时，Legates在那里，他发表了一场演讲，对美国极端降雨量的趋势不屑一顾，认为这是测量变化的产物。
Legates也是2018年心脏地带“重新考虑过的气候变化”报告的主要作者之一，该报告采用了政府间气候变化专门委员会(IPCC)的格式和外衣-甚至将其博客小圈子称为“非政府国际气候变化专门委员会”-但提出了长期以来被揭穿的无稽之谈。
例如，报告总结称，“没有令人信服的科学证据表明，全球平均气温或气候影响的长期趋势超出了自然变异性的界限。”这是多么明显的错误，怎么夸大都不为过。
四月，莱格茨在哈特兰研究所网站上合著了一篇关于新冠肺炎模特的博客文章，链接到一篇关于“夸大”死亡数字的阴谋论文章。
“至关重要的是，他们要重新检查模型和假设--”帖子中写道，“并区分新冠肺炎的死亡实际上是由病毒引起的……。不仅与此相关或与之相关，而且主要是由于年龄、肥胖、肺炎或其他问题。我们负担不起比这种疾病更糟糕的治疗方法，也负担不起长期致命的国家经济停摆，而这种停摆本来可以通过更新和修正模型来缩短。“
然后，该帖子转而批评气候模型，错误地声称它们“一直夸大了变暖”。它甚至重复了气候科学家“改变”温度数据的古老谣言。考虑到使者的NOAA职称中的“环境观测和预测”，这些观点当然是相关的。
与政府间气候变化专门委员会(IPCC)的声明相反，伯顿(2018年)报告称，来自世界各地的最高质量的沿海验潮仪显示，自20世纪20年代或之前没有加速上升的证据，因此也没有证据表明受到大气二氧化碳水平上升的影响。
(顺便说一句，伯顿的引用并不是一项同行评议的研究，而只是一个绘制验潮仪数据的网站。)。
2015年，莱格茨和其他几位哈特兰的常客发表了一篇论文，题为“模型为何火爆：不可还原的简单气候模型的结果”。他们的模型预测到2100年全球变暖不会超过1摄氏度，这与发表的研究形成了鲜明对比。这是因为这篇论文声称，地球气候对温室气体的敏感性远远低于气候科学家的估计。
根据一组科学家发表的回应，问题在于他们的模型一团糟。首先，它假设气候系统对增加的温室气体做出即时反应，对海洋的存在嗤之以鼻。控制他们模型的一些值是通过类比压控电子电路来选择的，因为他们认为系统太稳定了，没有明显的放大反馈。
但最令人难以置信的是，该论文拒绝将其模型与观测到的全球温度进行比较-因为模拟的温度远远低于实际温度。取而代之的是，他们根据精心挑选的周期创建了一个假设的全球气温记录，然后将成功的拟合作为他们模型准确性的证据。(真实的气候模型实际上与真实的温度数据相匹配。)。
莱格茨也是少数有资质的逆向投资者中的一员，他们被邀请参加政府听证会，以证明气候科学是“不稳定的”。2014年，他在参议院环境和公共工程委员会作证说，气候变化没有损害美国的农业。他声称，数据显示干旱没有变化，并批评气候模型的使用是科学工具。
他作证说：“过去发生的干旱很可能会再次发生，而且频率和强度可能相似；因此，与试图通过严厉的二氧化碳排放控制政策来缓解干旱相比，为它们的回归做好准备是一种更好的策略。”
他的大部分证词不是集中在科学上，而是集中在他所谓的“持不同政见者”的迫害上。像其他痴迷于推动证据不支持的结论的逆向论者一样，他把被拒绝的论文(以及对他作为州气候学家的地位的挑战)归因于他自己的错误，而是一场压制“独立思考”的阴谋。这种想象中的迫害为Legates赢得了2015年哈特兰研究所会议上的“捍卫科学勇气奖”。
类似以上的想法已经在政治家和表演委员会听证会上变得普遍，但现在他们将在NOAA的上层得到代表，NOAA是一个负责关键的实时监测和研究的机构。
当然，这是特朗普政府模式的一部分。周二，当特朗普总统就创纪录的野火与加州官员会面时，他无视人们对气温变暖这一不可避免的现实的担忧，他说，“天气会开始变凉，你就等着瞧吧。”当加州自然资源部长韦德·克劳福特(Wade CrowFoot)回应说，他希望科学同意时，特朗普补充说，“实际上，我不认为科学会知道。”
note_en:
The US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration recently hired a new person in an upper-level deputy assistant secretary position. Normally, this would not be too surprising or newsworthy, but this is an exception. Joining NOAA as the “Deputy Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Environmental Observation and Prediction” is University of Delaware Professor  David Legates—a well-known contrarian who rejects the science of human-caused climate change.
  The position apparently reports to acting head of NOAA Neil Jacobs, although the circumstances of the hire are unknown. Ars asked NOAA about the duties of this position, but the agency has not responded. Jacobs was entangled in the fallout from President Trump’s inaccurate tweets about Hurricane Dorian that culminated in a forecast map doctored with a black marker. A  pair of investigations found that Jacobs capitulated to directives from the office of Commerce Secretary Wilbur Ross and the White House, releasing an unsigned NOAA statement that sought to rescue the president’s inaccurate statements by mildly admonishing the forecasters who corrected him.
 The Washington Post  reported that Legates’ department at the University of Delaware informed students in an email that he would not be teaching in the fall semester while noting, “David hopes to be back at UD in the spring.”
   Legates was Delaware’s State Climatologist between 2005 and 2011. Although he started his career working on precipitation data and patterns, he is primarily known for rejecting, at every opportunity, the human role in climate change. He’s a frequent contributor to work by the Heartland Institute—a “think tank” that opposes the facts of climate science. When Ars  visited a Heartland conference in 2015, Legates was there, presenting a talk that waved away trends in US rainfall extremes as an artifact of measurement changes.
 Legates was also a lead author of a 2018 Heartland “Climate Change Reconsidered” report, which adopts the format and trappings of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change—even calling its coterie of bloggers the “Nongovernmental International Panel on Climate Change”—but presents long-debunked nonsense.
 For example, the report’s summary concludes, “There is no compelling scientific evidence of long-term trends in global mean temperatures or climate impacts that exceed the bounds of natural variability.” It  cannot be  overstated how  demonstrably false  that is.
  In April, Legates co-authored a  blog post on the Heartland Institute website about COVID-19 models that linked to a conspiratorial post about “inflated” death numbers.
 “It is vital that they recheck the models and assumptions—” the post reads, “and distinguish between COVID-19 deaths actually due to the virus... and not just associated with or compounded by it, but primarily due to age, obesity, pneumonia or other issues. We can’t afford a cure that’s worse than the disease—or a prolonged and deadly national economic shutdown that could have been shortened by updated and corrected models.”
  The post then turns to criticize climate models,  falsely claiming that they “have always overstated the warming.” It even repeats the ancient canard that climate scientists “alter” temperature data. These views are certainly relevant given the “Environmental Observation and Prediction” in Legates’ NOAA job title.
  Contrary to the IPCC’s statement that it is “very likely” sea-level rise is accelerating, Burton (2018) reports the highest quality coastal tide gauges from around the world show no evidence of acceleration since the 1920s or before, and therefore no evidence of being affected by rising atmospheric CO 2 levels.
 (That Burton citation is not a peer-reviewed study, by the way, but simply a website that plots tide-gauge data.)
 In 2015, Legates and several other Heartland regulars published a  paper titled “Why models run hot: results from an irreducibly simple climate model.” Their model projected no more than 1°C of global warming through the year 2100, in stark contrast to published research. This was because the paper claimed that the sensitivity of Earth’s climate to greenhouse gases was far lower than estimated by climate scientists.
 The problem, according to a  response published by a group of scientists, was that their model was a mess. For starters, it assumed that the climate system responded instantaneously to increased greenhouse gases, thumbing its nose at the existence of the ocean. And some of the values controlling their model were selected  by analogy to voltage-controlled electronic circuits, because they  believed the system was too stable to have significant amplifying feedbacks.
 But most unbelievably, the paper had declined to compare its model to observed global temperatures—because the modeled temperatures were far lower than reality. Instead, they  created a hypothetical global temperature record based on cherry-picked periods and then treated the successful fit as evidence of their model’s accuracy. (Real climate models  actually match real temperature data.)
   Legates is also in the tiny Rolodex of credentialed contrarians who get invited to government hearings to testify that climate science is “unsettled.” In 2014, he  testified before the Senate Environment and Public Works Committee that climate change is not harming agriculture in the United States. He claimed that the data shows no changes in drought and criticized the use of climate models as scientific tools.
 “Droughts that have happened in the past are likely to occur again, and with likely similar frequencies and intensities; thus, preparation for their return is a better strategy than trying to mitigate them through draconian CO 2 emission control policies,” he testified.
 Much of his testimony focused not science but on his supposed persecution for “dissent.” Like other contrarians obsessed with pushing conclusions that the evidence does not support, he chalks up the rejected papers (and challenges to his status as state climatologist), not to his own mistakes, but to a conspiracy to squash “independent thinking.” This imagined persecution  earned Legates a “Courage in Defense of Science Award” at that 2015 Heartland Institute conference.
 Ideas like the above have become common from politicians and performative committee hearings, but they’ll now be represented at the upper level of NOAA—an agency tasked with critical real-time monitoring and research.
 This is part of a pattern under the Trump administration, of course. While meeting with California officials about record-setting wildfires on Tuesday, President Trump disregarded concerns about the inescapable reality of warming temperatures,  saying, “It’ll start getting cooler, you just watch.” When California Secretary for Natural Resources Wade Crowfoot responded that he wished science agreed, Trump added, “I don’t think science knows, actually.”
title: 气候科学逆向投资者被安排在NOAA高层职位
title_en: Climate science contrarian installed in upper-level NOAA position

note:
今天，除了MacOS Big Sur之外，苹果发布了其操作系统的大部分主要年度更新。IOS14、iPadOS14、TVOS14和WatchOS7将在今天和明天全天(以及明天，在一些地区)面向全球用户推出。
这一次的时机不同寻常。通常情况下，苹果在9月份发布产品直播流几天后发布操作系统更新，通常与新款iPhone的发布时间非常接近。但由于本周没有发布iPhone，苹果在发布会后仅一天就推出了这些更新，而在新款Apple Watch和第八代iPad预计最终到达用户手中的前一天。
IOS 14为iPhone的主屏幕和应用程序管理体验带来了主要的新功能，支持去年在iPadOS中引入的主屏幕小工具，以及一个新的App Library视图，允许在一个面板上更轻松地管理所有已安装的应用程序。随之而来的是对视频的全系统画中画支持-另一个从iPadOS发展而来的功能-以及对消息、地图和其他应用程序的一些重大改进，以及一个新的实时翻译应用程序。
WatchOS7将锻炼应用程序重新命名为Fitness，并增加了新的力量训练功能等。操作系统还增加了一项功能，可以帮助用户确保正确洗手，并保持足够的时间。也许最重要的是，watchOS7提供了睡眠跟踪功能，这是许多竞争对手的健身可穿戴设备上提供的一项主要功能，但Apple Watch没有。
与去年发布的版本相比，iPadOS14扩展了窗口小部件的功能，并且在整个操作系统中进行了一些小的设计优化。TVOS 14扩展了家庭功能和多用户支持，并增加了画中画支持。
对于那些叫嚣着要新功能的消费者来说，即时发布的时间似乎是一个积极的消息，但它对开发者来说却是一种高度的颠覆性。这些每年发布的主要操作系统通常要求开发人员对他们的应用程序进行更改，要么是为了符合新的要求，要么是为了支持操作系统或新发布的设备中添加的功能。开发人员已经访问了引入这些功能并进行了几个月调整的测试版--准确地说，是8个测试版。
但最终测试和提交准备通常发生在黄金主更新播种给开发人员的时候。金牌大师的意图是与操作系统的最终公开版本几乎完全匹配，特别是为了测试和提交的目的。
但由于这些发布的时机，开发者在公开发布之前只有不到一整天的时间与黄金大师在一起，导致许多深夜，延迟发布，并发出沮丧的声音。
在一片混乱之中，苹果公司(Apple)昨日在其开发者门户网站上发布了一份报告，恳请开发者确保在iOS 14和iPadOS 14向全球客户推出时，你的应用程序已经准备就绪。这份报告并没有承认，考虑到时间框架，对许多人来说，这几乎是不可能的。
出于这个原因，用户应该会在发布时看到iOS 14和iPadOS 14中的第三方应用程序出现更多问题，他们应该会预计与新版本相关的一些更新将比今年晚几天到来。
和往常一样，我们正在对新版本进行审查--预计下周晚些时候会看到我们对iOS14和iPadOS14的审查。
note_en:
Today marks the launch of most of Apple's major annual updates to its operating systems—excepting macOS Big Sur. iOS 14, iPadOS 14, tvOS 14, and watchOS 7 are rolling out to users across the globe and throughout the day today (and tomorrow, in some regions).
 The timing is unusual this time around. Normally, Apple launches OS updates several days after its September product-announcement livestream, typically timed closely with the release of new iPhones. But with no iPhones announced this week, Apple instead pushed these updates only one day after the event and one day before the new Apple Watch and eighth-generation iPad are expected to end up in users' hands.
 iOS 14 brings major new features to the home screen and app management experience for the iPhone, with support for the home screen widgets that were introduced in iPadOS last year and a new App Library view that allows for easier managing of all installed apps on one panel. Along with that comes systemwide picture-in-picture support for videos—another feature that has made its way from iPadOS—and a number of significant improvements to Messages, Maps, and other applications, as well as a new live translation app.
 watchOS 7 renames the Workouts app to "Fitness" and doubles down on new strength-training features, among other things. The OS also adds a feature that helps users make sure they are washing their hands correctly and for an adequate amount of time. Perhaps most importantly, watchOS 7 brings sleep tracking—a major feature available in many competing fitness wearables but not the Apple Watch.
 iPadOS 14 expands the widgets feature compared to last year's release, and it features a number of small design optimizations throughout the operating system. And tvOS 14 expands Home functionality and multi-user support and adds picture-in-picture support.
  The immediate timing of the releases might seem like a positive for consumers who were clamoring for new features, but it's been highly disruptive to developers. These major annual OS releases often require developers to make changes to their apps, either to comply with new requirements or to support features added to the OS or newly launched devices. Developers have had access to beta releases that have been introducing these features and making tweaks for a few months—eight beta releases, to be precise.
 But final testing and preparation for submission typically happens when the golden master update is seeded to developers. The golden master is intended to be an almost-exact-match with the final public release of the operating system, specifically for this purpose of testing and submission.
 But with the timing of these launches, developers had less than a full day with the golden master before the public release, leading to many late nights, delayed releases, and  vocal  frustration.
 Amidst the chaos, Apple  published a note to its developer portal yesterday imploring developers to "Make sure your apps are ready when iOS 14 and iPadOS 14 become available to customers worldwide." That note came with no acknowledgement that, for many, that would be nigh impossible given the timeframe.
 For that reason, users should expect to see more issues with third-party apps in iOS 14 and iPadOS 14 at launch, and they should expect some updates related to the new release to come several days later than usual this year.
 As always, we are working on a review of the new release—expect to see our review of iOS 14 and iPadOS 14 late next week.
title: 苹果仅提前一天就发布了iOS 14、iPadOS 14、TVOS 14和WatchOS 7
title_en: Apple launches iOS 14, iPadOS 14, tvOS 14, and watchOS 7 on just one day’s notice

今天，唐纳德·特朗普总统提名他的一名政府官员担任联邦通信委员会(Federal Communications Commission)成员，试图推动他提出的打击社交媒体网站的提议。
特朗普宣布提名内森·西明顿(Nathan Simington)，他目前是美国国家电信和信息管理局(NTIA)的高级顾问。正如“边缘报”(The Verge)上周报道的那样，西明顿在该机构的社交媒体监管议程中发挥了重要作用，当时有消息称，特朗普正在考虑西明顿担任联邦通信委员会(FCC)的职位。
西明顿将取代共和党人迈克尔·里利(Michael Rielly)，后者显然激怒了特朗普，后者表示，联邦通信委员会必须维护适用于企业实体的第一修正案言论保护，特别是当它们参与编辑决策时。里利的言论表明，他不太可能支持特朗普政府提交的由NTIA提交的请愿书，该请愿书要求联邦通信委员会重新解释“通讯体面法”(Communications Decency Act)第230条，以限制社交媒体平台的合法使用。这份请愿书要求联邦通信委员会重新解释“通讯体面法”第230条，以限制社交媒体平台的合法使用。里利的言论表明，他不太可能支持特朗普政府提交的请愿书。该请愿书要求联邦通信委员会重新解释“通讯体面法”第230条，以限制社交媒体平台的合法使用。
据“华盛顿邮报”报道，西明顿参与起草了特朗普5月份的行政命令，鼓励联邦通信委员会(Federal Communications Commission)重新考虑“通信体面法案”(Communications Decency Act)第230条如何保护社交媒体平台免受其托管内容的责任。
特朗普曾在3月份提名O&#39；Rielly连任联邦通信委员会的另一个5年任期，但在O&#39；Rielly发表评论后撤回了连任提名。西明顿的提名需要得到参议院的批准。如果参议院在此之前不对提名采取行动，里利可能会在委员会任职到2020年底，但如果西明顿正式获得这一职位，他将不得不离职。O&#39；里利的FCC任期技术上已于2019年6月到期。
特朗普对推特和脸书对他的帖子的处理方式感到愤怒，他需要在由五名成员组成的FCC中获得三票才能让NTIA的请愿书获得批准。两名民主党人表示反对，称这份请愿书等同于联邦通信委员会成为总统的演讲警察。
特朗普得到了共和党联邦通信委员会委员布兰登·卡尔(Brendan Carr)的支持，后者曾多次明确支持特朗普对社交媒体的打压，并批评推特(Twitter)对特朗普声称的邮寄选票将存在严重舞弊行为进行事实核查。
问号是董事长阿吉特·派(Ajit Pai)，他没有公开参与特朗普政府的请愿活动。帕伊正在就请愿书发表公开评论，并呼吁展开激烈的辩论。特朗普愿意更换O&#39；里利表明，如果帕伊不听从白宫的要求，他可能会尝试对帕伊做同样的事情。
如果乔·拜登(Joe Biden)赢得总统大选，民主党人将重新获得FCC多数席位。如果特朗普赢得连任，派可能会继续留在联邦通信委员会，但联邦通信委员会主席通常会在总统任期结束时辞职，即使白宫没有易手。一些细节使事情变得复杂-派的任期延长到2021年年中，如果参议院没有提名和批准任何继任者，他可能会继续任职到2022年。但是，如果特朗普连任，而派不是自愿离开的，那么一旦派的任期在2021年年中正式届满，特朗普就有机会取代派。
在奥巴马政府期间，帕伊声称白宫策划了联邦通信委员会实施网络中立规则，并表示，我们不应该成为白宫做出的政治决定的橡皮图章。特朗普取代了里利，在试图制定联邦通信委员会政策的过程中发挥了更直接的作用，但帕伊并没有公开批评特朗普，就像他对奥巴马总统的批评一样。
note_en:
President Donald Trump today nominated one of his administration officials to serve on the Federal Communications Commission in an attempt to push through his proposed crackdown on social media websites.
 Trump  announced the nomination of Nathan Simington, who is currently a senior advisor in the National Telecommunications and Information Administration (NTIA). Simington "played a significant role in the agency's social media regulation agenda," as  The Verge reported last week when news broke that Trump was considering Simington for the FCC position.
 Simington would replace Republican Michael O'Rielly, who  apparently angered Trump by saying that the FCC must uphold First Amendment speech protections "that apply to corporate entities, especially when they engage in editorial decision making." O'Rielly's comments signaled that he isn't likely to support the Trump administration petition, submitted by the NTIA, that asks the FCC to reinterpret Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act in order to limit social media platforms' legal protections for hosting third-party content when the platforms take down or alter content they consider objectionable.
 "Simington had a hand in drafting Trump's May executive order encouraging the Federal Communications Commission to rethink how Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act protects social media platforms from liability for the content it hosts,"  according to The Washington Post.
 Trump had nominated O'Rielly to another five-year FCC term in March but withdrew the re-nomination after O'Rielly's comments. Simington's nomination would need approval from the Senate. O'Rielly could serve on the commission through the end of 2020 if the Senate doesn't act on the nomination before then, but he  would have to leave if Simington officially gets the job. O'Rielly's FCC term technically expired in June 2019.
  Trump, who is angry about  Twitter and Facebook's treatment of his posts, needs three votes on the five-member FCC to get the NTIA petition approved. The two Democrats oppose it, saying the petition amounts to the FCC becoming the president's "speech police."
 Trump has the support of Republican FCC Commissioner Brendan Carr, who has explicitly and repeatedly  supported Trump's social media crackdown and criticized Twitter for  adding a fact check to Trump's claims that mail-in ballots will be "substantially fraudulent."
 The question mark is Chairman Ajit Pai, who hasn't weighed in publicly on the Trump administration petition. Pai is taking public comments on the petition and called for a " vigorous debate." Trump's willingness to replace O'Rielly suggests that he could try to do the same with Pai if the chair doesn't do the White House's bidding.
 If Joe Biden wins the presidency, Democrats would retake the FCC majority. Pai could stay on the FCC if Trump wins re-election, but FCC chairs usually step down at the end of a presidential term even if the White House hasn't changed hands. A few details complicate matters—Pai's term extends until mid-2021, and he could continue serving through 2022 if no replacement is nominated and approved by the Senate. But if Trump gets a second term and Pai doesn't leave of his own volition, Trump would have a chance to replace Pai once Pai's term officially expires in mid-2021.
 During the Obama administration, Pai  claimed that the White House orchestrated the FCC's imposition of net neutrality rules and said, "We shouldn't be a rubber stamp for political decisions made by the White House." Trump is taking a much more direct role in trying to set FCC policy by replacing O'Rielly, but Pai has not publicly made the same criticism of Trump that he made of President Obama.
title: 特朗普更换FCC成员，以推动Twitter/Facebook的打击
title_en: Trump replaces FCC member in bid to push through Twitter/Facebook crackdown

note:
作为今天PlayStation5展示的一部分，索尼最终公布了PlayStation5的发布价格：标准版499.99美元(欧元499.99，GB 449.99，人民币49,980元)或全数字版(或欧元399.99，GB 359.99，人民币39,980元)。
这两款游戏机将于11月12日在美国、日本、加拿大、墨西哥、澳大利亚、新西兰和韩国发布，随后将于11月19日在世界其他地区发布。索尼在活动后表示，从9月16日(星期四)开始，部分零售商将开始接受预订。
499美元的价格与微软Xbox Series X在下一代游戏机高端的定价不相上下。然而，全数字版的售价比同样没有碟片的Xbox Series S高出100美元，这也是X系列在硬件能力上的一个台阶。
索尼还发布了PlayStation Plus系列，精选了一些备受瞩目的、定义了一代人的PS4游戏，作为PS5独家福利提供标准PlayStation Plus会员资格。目前上映的阵容包括：“战神”、“血统”、“捉妖记”、“最终幻想XV”、“Fallout 4”、“凡人Kombat 10”、“棘轮与叮当”、“Days Gone”、“Uncharted 4”、“Uncharted 4”、“直到黎明”、“Battlefield 1”、“臭名昭著：次子”、“蝙蝠侠：雅克罕姆·骑士”、“最后的守护者”、“The Last Of Us Remasted”、“角色5”以及“生化危机”。
广场埃尼克斯的“最终幻想十六”的镜头引发了一系列游戏预告片。新游戏的特点显然是实时的战斗，看起来很大程度上受到了“魔鬼五月哭泣”的启发，敌人直接来自黑暗灵魂及其同类。悲情中也充斥着剑和巫术的场景和人物，吟诵着这样的台词，比如我们不质疑命令-我们听从他们的命令，如果这是我做的最后一件事，我会杀了你，绝对没有讽刺意味。这款游戏将是PlayStation上的独家游戏机，也可以在PC上使用。
目前还没有宣布目标上映日期，但我们会提醒你，从宣布到最终上映，整整有10年的时间。
蜘蛛侠的新镜头：迈尔斯·莫拉莱斯强调了一起事故，除了标准的网络诽谤之外，这位名义上的英雄还获得了发光的、基于电力的力量。打斗场面让我们想起了蜘蛛侠，就像“臭名昭著”这样的游戏。PS5的发布标题也将在PS4上提供。
一款受哈利波特启发的新游戏“霍格沃茨遗产”发生在19世纪末版的霍格沃茨魔法学校，现在是时候把你的故事添加到这些神圣的大厅里了。预告片暗示了一个更大的谜团，这将需要离开学校，用你新发现的魔法能力来揭示你所代表的……。你现在所做的选择将定义霍格沃茨的遗产。请在2021年寻找它。
新的死亡环路镜头展示了更多受跑酷启发的第一人称枪战。预告片还强调了这款游戏的八个目标如何拥有相互关联的故事，这些故事将通过同一天的重复循环来揭示。玩家将不得不安排事情让多个目标聚集在一个地方，以便在游戏循环的严格时间限制下更有效地杀戮。这款游戏将于2021年第二季度推出PlayStation独家游戏机(PC版)。
“恶魔的灵魂”的主角是一个身穿中世纪盔甲的人，他在黑暗而安静的城堡大厅里游荡，用他的剑砍杀活着的和不死的敌人，这是你很可能会想到的。预告片中有一些出色的灯光效果，从墙上的火炬到喷火的龙，再到月光透过敞开的城堡烛台。这款游戏将在PS5上发布，有点令人惊讶的是，它也将在PC上推出。
展览结束时，简短地调侃了2019年翻拍的“战神”续集。克瑞托斯警告说，你必须做好准备，因为拉格纳洛克2021年就要来了。然而，没有播放任何游戏或场景镜头。
弗雷迪的一款新的五夜游戏“安全突破口”，以怪异的机器人声音为特色，试图引诱你在一个闪亮的家庭娱乐中心环境中玩耍。短片预告片中没有显示实际的游戏过程，也没有讨论发布日期。
魔鬼五月哭泣5：特别版是魔鬼五月哭泣5的特别版，将在PS5推出时疯狂地推出。
职责召唤黑色行动冷战的多人阿尔法将从9月1日(星期五)起在PlayStation4上独家提供。18号到20号。
索尼不可能通过移除磁盘驱动器来节省100美元。这肯定是他们用来试图与超级便宜的S系列赛竞争的亏损领头羊。我认为微软在这一代玩得很好。这并不是说太重要，因为所有的独家产品都将进入个人电脑。
考虑到没有光驱也意味着没有二手市场可供竞争，也没有零售商从销售额中分得一杯羹
note_en:
As part of  a PlayStation 5 showcase today, Sony  finally revealed the launch pricing for the PlayStation 5: $499.99 for the standard edition (€499.99, £449.99, ¥49,980) or $399 for the  "All-Digital" version without a disc drive (or €399.99, £359.99, ¥39,980).
 Both consoles will launch on November 12 in the United States, Japan, Canada, Mexico, Australia, New Zealand, and South Korea, followed by a November 19 launch in the rest of the world. Pre-orders will be available at select retailers starting Thursday, September 16, Sony said after the event.
 The $499 price matches  Microsoft's Xbox Series X pricing on the top end of the next console generation. The All Digital Edition, however, comes in at $100 more than the similarly discless Xbox Series S, which is also a step down in hardware power from the Series X.
    Sony also announced the PlayStation Plus collection, a selection of high-profile, "generation-defining" PS4 games available as a PS5-exclusive benefit with a standard PlayStation Plus membership. Included in the lineup shown:  God of War, Bloodborne, Monster Hunter World, Final Fantasy XV, Fallout 4, Mortal Kombat 10, Ratchet and Clank, Days Gone, Uncharted 4, Until Dawn, Detroit: Become Human, Battlefield 1, Infamous: Second Son, Batman: Arkham Knight, The Last Guardian, The Last of Us Remastered, Persona 5, and  Resident Evil: Biohazard.
  Footage of Square Enix's  Final Fantasy XVI led off a series of game trailers. The new game features apparently real-time battles that look heavily inspired by the like of  Devil May Cry, with enemies straight out of  Dark Souls and its ilk. The sword-and-sorcery setting and characters pack on the pathos, too, intoning lines like "We do not question orders—we follow them," and "I'll kill you if it's the last thing I do" with absolutely no irony. The game will be a console-exclusive on PlayStation and will also be available on PC.
 No target release date was announced, but we'll remind you that 10 full years elapsed between the announcement and eventual release of  Final Fantasy XV.
    New footage of  Spider-Man: Miles Morales highlights an accident that gives the titular hero glowing, electricity-based powers in addition to the standard webslinging. The fight scenes reminded us of   Spider-Man by way of a game like   Infamous. The PS5 launch title will also be available on the PS4.
 A new  Harry Potter-inspired game,  Hogwarts Legacy, takes place in a late 1800s version of Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry, where "now is the time to add your story to these hallowed halls." The trailer hints at a larger mystery that will require leaving the school and using your newfound wizarding powers to "reveal what you stand for... The choices you make now will define the legacy of Hogwarts." Look for it in 2021.
 New  Deathloop footage shows off some more of the parkour-inspired, telekentic first-person gunplay. The trailer also highlights how the game's eight targets have interlocking stories that will be revealed through repeated loops through the same day. Players will have to arrange things to get multiple targets to come together in one place, for more efficient killing in the game loop's strict time limit. The game is coming as a PlayStation console exclusive (with a PC version) in the second quarter of 2021.
    Demon's Souls features a man in medieval armor wandering dark, eerily quiet castle halls and slashing both living and undead enemies with his sword, as you might well expect. Some excellent lighting effects were in play during the trailer, from torches on walls to fire-breathing dragons to rays of moonlight filtering through open castle sconces. The game will be available at launch on the PS5 and, somewhat surprisingly, will also be coming to PC.
 The showcase concluded with a brief tease of a sequel to 2019's  God of War remake. "You must prepare yourself" Kratos warns, because "Ragnarok is coming" in 2021. No gameplay or cutscene footage was shown, however.
  A new Five Nights at Freddy's game,  Security Breach, features an eerie robotic voice trying to lure you out to play in a shiny family fun center environment. No actual gameplay was shown or release date discussed in the short teaser trailer.
  Devil May Cry 5: Special Edition is a special edition of  Devil May Cry 5 that will be available dgitially when the PS5 launches.
 The multiplayer alpha of Call of Duty Black Ops Cold War will be available exclusive on PlayStation 4 from Friday, Sept. 18 through the 20th.
  There is no way Sony is saving $100 by removing the disk drive. That's gotta be a loss leader they're using to try and compete with the super cheap Series S.  I think MS played this gen pretty well. Not that it matter too much, since all the 'exclusives' are coming to PC.
   Consider that no disc drive also means no pre-owned market to compete with, and no retailers taking a cut of sales
title: PlayStation5活动：11月12日，499美元(含光驱)，399美元(不含)
title_en: PlayStation 5 event: $499 with a disc drive, $399 without, on Nov. 12

note:
周二，苹果在一份新的法庭文件中抨击Epic Games，声称这家堡垒之夜的制造商发起诉讼，纯粹是为了引起人们对这款游戏的兴趣。
由于与史诗公司对苹果的指控无关的原因，堡垒之夜的人气正在走下坡路。截至2020年7月，对堡垒之夜的兴趣较2019年10月减少近七成。苹果在文件中表示，这起诉讼(以及它引发的头条新闻)似乎是旨在重振人们对堡垒之夜的兴趣的营销活动的一部分。
上个月苹果与Epic Games的大战爆发后，堡垒之夜发起了一场针对苹果的运动，其中包括#FreeFortnite标签和一项锦标赛，玩家可以在比赛中赢得反苹果的奖品。它还发布了一段恶搞苹果著名广告的视频。
苹果70%的数据基于谷歌趋势的数据，该数据显示，自2017年堡垒之夜发布以来，有多少人在该网站上搜索。
它还援引了雅虎财经4月份的一份报告，该报告称，一项调查发现，其他战斗皇室游戏的受欢迎程度正在追赶堡垒之夜，以及彭博社2019年8月的一份报告，该报告称堡垒之夜的收入正在下滑。
苹果的申请是与Etic就是否应该允许堡垒之夜重新出现在应用商店上的争斗的一部分。这场争斗的一部分是苹果与Etic就是否应该允许堡垒之夜重返应用商店展开的斗争的一部分。苹果将堡垒之夜踢出了应用商店，原因是Etic故意规避了应用商店对应用内支付的规定。
要在App Store上运行，应用程序必须使用苹果的支付系统，并支付15%至30%的佣金。EPIC用自己的支付系统取代了苹果的支付系统。
EPIC试图申请临时限制令，迫使苹果公司恢复堡垒之夜的职务，但这一请求被法官驳回，法官表示，EPIC没有证明被踢出应用商店会造成不可弥补的损害。法官还表示，Epicc违反与苹果合同的决定是经过深思熟虑的。
苹果在其新提交的文件中抨击Epici向法院寻求帮助。Etic纵火，并向其倒汽油，现在请求法院紧急协助扑灭，尽管Etic自己只需遵守多年来一直有利可图地支配其与苹果公司关系的合同条款，就可以立即做到这一点，该公司表示。在过去的几年里，Epicc一直在管理其与苹果的关系，这些条款一直有利可图，但现在它请求法院紧急协助扑灭这场大火，尽管Etic自己可以立即做到这一点。
苹果表示，如果Etic恢复使用苹果的支付系统，即使继续在法庭上打官司，它也可以很容易地重返App Store。该公司表示，“史诗”本身只需按一下键盘，就可以避免对自身、堡垒之夜玩家或第三方造成任何进一步的伤害。“史诗”称，“史诗”只需按一下键盘，就可以避免对自身、对玩家或第三方造成任何进一步的伤害。
EPIC并不是唯一一家对该规定提出异议的开发商：包括Spotify在内的其他主要开发商都表示，这种做法是反竞争的，该系统是欧盟反垄断调查的对象。

note_en:
Apple attacked Epic Games in a  new court filing on Tuesday, claiming the maker of "Fortnite" launched a lawsuit over in-app payments solely to generate interest in the game.
  "For reasons having nothing to do with Epic's claims against Apple, Fortnite's popularity is on the wane. By July 2020, interest in Fortnite had decreased by nearly 70% as compared to October 2019. This lawsuit (and the front-page headlines it has generated) appears to be part of a marketing campaign designed to reinvigorate interest in Fortnite," Apple said in the filing.
 After the battle between Apple and Epic Games exploded last month, "Fortnite" launched a campaign against Apple including a #FreeFortnite hashtag and a  tournament in which players could win anti-Apple prizes. It also released a video parodying Apple's famous "1984" ad.
  Apple based its 70% figure on  Google Trends data showing how many people searched for "Fortnite" since its launch in 2017.
   It also cited an April report from  Yahoo Finance about a survey that found other battle-royale games were catching up to "Fortnite" in popularity, as well as an August 2019 report from  Bloomberg that said revenue for "Fortnite" was sliding.
 Apple's filing is part of a  battle with Epic over whether "Fortnite" should be allowed back on the App Store. Apple kicked "Fortnite" off the App Store after Epic deliberately sidestepped App Store rules over in-app payments.
 To run on the App Store, apps must use Apple's payment system and hand over a commission of 15% to 30%. Epic replaced Apple's payment system with its own.
 Epic tried to file a temporary restraining order to force Apple to reinstate "Fortnite." This plea was  rejected August 25 by the judge, who said Epic had not demonstrated that getting kicked off the App Store would result in "irreparable harm." The judge also said Epic's decision to breach its contract with Apple was deliberate.
   Apple laid into Epic in its new filing for asking the court for help. "Epic started a fire, and poured gasoline on it, and now asks this Court for emergency assistance in putting it out, even though Epic can do so itself in an instant by simply adhering to the contractual terms that have profitably governed its relationship with Apple for years," it said.
 Apple said Epic could easily get back onto the App Store if it reverted to using Apple's payment system, even while continuing to fight in court. "Epic itself can avoid any further harm — to itself, 'Fortnite' players, or third parties — with a keystroke," it said.
  Epic isn't the only developer to take issue with the rule: Other major players including  Spotify have said the practice is anticompetitive, and the system is  the subject of an antitrust investigation in the European Union.

title: 苹果称‘堡垒之夜’炒作正在消退Epic Games打官司的唯一原因是为了引起轰动
title_en: Apple says 'Fortnite' hype is fading and the only reason Epic Games started a legal battle was to generate buzz

他说：“世界上有14亿人没有银行账户。在这14亿人中，有近13亿是女性。“创始人兼首席执行官艾玛·史密斯告诉我。
在许多方面，银行账户都是由男性设计的，也是为男性设计的。即使你看一下金融科技创业公司，他们中的大多数都有男性创始人。拉丁美洲已经有几家挑战者银行，如Nubank、Banco Inter、Banco Original和UALá。但大多数挑战者银行专注于成熟市场，如欧洲和美国。史密斯认为，瞄准新兴国家的女性代表着一个巨大的市场机遇。
Jefa仔细研究了拉丁美洲女性经常没有银行账户或对银行账户不满意的原因。
例如，银行经常要求你持有最低余额，尽管从统计上看，女性的收入低于男性。银行告诉你来分行开户，尽管很多家庭只有一辆车，坐公交车可能会很麻烦。银行的产品过于混乱，不会投资于针对女性的营销渠道。
史密斯说：“正是出于这些原因，我们认为我们需要一种无分支的全数字化解决方案。”“我们没有最低余额要求；你只需要一张政府发放的身份证，三四分钟就可以报名了。”
当Jefa在几个月后推出时，开户将是免费的。几天后你会得到一个账户和一张卡。这项服务有一个内置的储蓄功能，可以让你收集购买的商品并设定目标。
将会有一个名为“做女人是值得的”的奖励计划。根据您的购买情况，您可以在卫生用品、去看妇科医生等方面获得积分。
首先，您可以将这些点数转换为现金。后来，你可以想象在对女性重要的地方兑换积分。
Jefa用户将能够在应用程序内发送和接收免费的点对点支付。当涉及到提取和充值您的账户时，Jefa正在建立一个商家网络来安全地管理现金。
该公司还在开发一个信用建设平台，其功能应该有点像Chime的同等功能。
Jefa将首先在中美洲推出，首先是哥斯达黎加和危地马拉。已经有5万人在等待名单上。该公司知道围绕其产品建立一个社区是很重要的。所以你可以期待一个社区论坛，这样你就可以和其他Jefa用户讨论财务问题。
银行的名声往往不好，因为它们是没有灵魂的实体，不一定了解你的需求。当他们不断告诉你你不符合标准时，你可能会感到沮丧。创建数字优先银行对垂直银行来说是一个机遇。而Jefa就是一个很好的例子。
note_en:
“There are 1.4 billion people in the world without a bank account. Out of those 1.4 billion, nearly 1.3 billion are women,” founder and CEO  Emma Smith   told me.
 In many ways, bank accounts have been designed by men and for men. Even if you look at fintech startups, most of them have male founders. There is already a handful of challenger banks in Latin America, such as  Nubank,  Banco Inter,  Banco Original and  Ualá. But most challenger banks focus on mature markets, such as Europe and the U.S. Smith thinks that targeting women in emerging countries represents a huge market opportunity.
   Jefa   has carefully examined the reasons why women in Latin America often don’t have bank accounts or are unsatisfied with their bank accounts.
 For instance, banks often ask you to hold a minimum balance even though women statistically earn less than men. Banks tell you to come to a branch to open an account even though many families only have one car and taking the bus can be a hassle. Banks have overly confusing products and don’t invest in marketing channels for women.
 “It’s for all those reasons that we thought we need a fully digital solution that is branchless,” Smith said. “We have no minimum balance requirement; all you need is a government-issued ID and you can sign up in three or four minutes.”
 When Jefa launches in a few months, opening an account will be free. You get an account and a card a few days later. The service has a built-in savings feature that lets you round up purchases and set goals.
 There will be a reward program called “It pays to be a woman.” Based on your purchases, you’ll earn points on hygiene products, going to the gynecologist, etc.
  At first, you’ll be able to convert those points to cash back. Later, you could imagine redeeming your points at places that matter to women.
 Jefa users will be able to send and receive free peer-to-peer payments within the app. And when it comes to withdrawing and topping up your account, Jefa is building a network of merchants to securely manage cash.
 The company is also working on a credit building platform that should work a bit like Chime’s  equivalent feature.
 Jefa will be launching first in Central America, starting with Costa Rica and Guatemala. There are already 50,000 people on the waiting list. The company knows that it’ll be important to build a community around its product. So you can expect a community forum so that you can discuss finance with other Jefa users.
 Banks tend to have a bad reputation because they are soulless entities that don’t necessarily understand your needs. It can be frustrating when they keep telling you that you don’t meet the criteria. Creating a digital-first bank represents an opportunity for vertical banks. And Jefa is a good example of that.
title: 对于没有银行账户的女性来说，jefa是一家具有挑战性的银行。
title_en: Jefa is a challenger bank for women without a bank account

在这场恰如其分的虚拟活动上，马克·扎克伯格宣布，Facebook将于明年推出“增强现实眼镜之路的下一步”，这是一款不具备增强现实功能的智能眼镜。
扎克伯格还宣布，Facebook将引入奢侈眼镜巨头Luxottica，新的消费设备将拥有Ray-ban品牌。扎克伯格表示，与Luxottica的合作将有助于适应人们对这些设备可能有的一系列风格和偏好。
扎克伯格说：“我们今天还没有产品与大家分享，但我很兴奋地告诉大家，我们已经形成了多年的合作伙伴关系，从明年开始制造和发布我们的第一副智能眼镜。”该公司几乎没有提供眼镜将具有哪些功能和不将具有哪些功能的细节，尽管该公司向TechCrunch证实，它将不会有显示器。其结果可能不会那么像谷歌眼镜，而更像是Snapchat于2016年首次发布的眼镜。
在研究方面，Facebook正在寻求制造可穿戴的AR眼镜，并将其与自己的软件集成在一起。2018年，该公司证实确实在制造自己的适当增强现实眼镜。Facebook增强现实部门负责人菲克斯·柯克帕特里克(Ficus Kirkpatrick)当时告诉TechCrunch：“我们希望看到这些眼镜成为现实，我认为我们希望发挥自己的作用，帮助将它们带到那里。”
从本月开始，少数经过“特殊训练”的Facebook员工和合同工将在校园内佩戴高科技眼镜，在现实世界条件下测试可穿戴研究设备。这项工作是该公司在Facebook现实实验室内的AR研究计划Aria项目的一部分。
在一段制作的视频中，Facebook描述了AR眼镜的一些理论用途：获得街道方向的覆盖，唱片商店的音乐推荐，甚至是视觉提醒，告诉你丢失的钥匙去了哪里。这段视频并不是该设备的演示，而是为了展示相关研究。
这些测试人员将帮助Facebook理解实验室无法弄清楚的事情，比如哪些传感器需要进入硬件，眼镜应该收集哪些数据，不应该收集哪些数据。
尽管Facebook自身也存在隐私丑闻，但它似乎强烈地回忆起谷歌眼镜(Google Glass)早期的坎坷岁月。谷歌眼镜是一款领先于时代的产品，其发布受到了用户对该设备内置摄像头的隐私担忧的影响。
Facebook Reality Labs负责人安德鲁·博斯沃思(Andrew Bosworth)很快强调，Facebook将在任何消费者AR设备上市之前进行广泛的测试。博斯沃思指出，Project Aria眼镜的测试版并不是一个正常工作的原型，他称它是“工作AR的前身”，没有功能平视显示器。
note_en:
At the fittingly virtual event, Mark Zuckerberg announced that Facebook would launch “the next step on the road to augmented reality glasses” next year, a pair of smart glasses that falls short of AR capability.
 Zuckerberg also announced that Facebook will bring in luxury eyewear giant Luxottica, and the new consumer device will have Ray-Ban branding. Zuckerberg suggested that the Luxottica partnership would help accommodate an array of styles and preferences that people might have for the devices.
  “We don’t have a product yet to share with you today but I am excited to share that we have formed a multi-year partnership starting with building and releasing our first pair of smart glasses next year,” Zuckerberg said. The company provided little detail on what capabilities the glasses would and would not have, though confirmed to TechCrunch that it would not have a display. The result will likely be less like Google Glass and more along the lines of  Snapchat’s Spectacles, first released in 2016.
 On the research side, Facebook is trucking along in its quest to build wearable AR glasses that will integrate with its own software. In 2018, the company confirmed that it was indeed building its own proper augmented reality glasses. “We want to see those glasses come into reality, and I think we want to play our part in helping to bring them there,” Facebook’s head of augmented reality Ficus Kirkpatrick  told TechCrunch at the time.
 Beginning this month, a handful of “specially trained” Facebook employees and contract workers will be sporting high tech glasses around campus to test wearable research devices in real world conditions. That effort is part of  Project Aria, the company’s AR research initiative within Facebook Reality Labs.
 In a produced video that wasn’t a demo of the device but was meant to show off relevant research, Facebook depicted some theoretical uses for AR glasses: getting an overlay of street directions, music recommendations in a record store or even visual alert showing you where your lost keys went.
    Those testers will help Facebook understand things it can’t figure out in the lab, like what sensors need to go into the hardware and what kind of data the glasses should and shouldn’t collect.
 Its own privacy scandals notwithstanding, Facebook seems to keenly recall the rocky early days of Google Glass, an ahead-of-its-time product with a launch marred by user privacy concerns over the device’s built-in camera.
 Andrew Bosworth, Head of Facebook Reality Labs, was quick to emphasize that Facebook will undergo extensive testing well before any consumer AR device would go on sale. Bosworth noted that a test version of Project Aria glasses isn’t a working prototype, calling it a “precursor to working AR” that doesn’t have a functional heads-up display.
title: Facebook将在2021年推出智能眼镜，这是它向AR设备迈出的“下一步”
title_en: Facebook is launching smart glasses in 2021, its ‘next step’ to an AR device

联合创始人马修·德沃曼(Matthew Dworman)和高拉夫·阿萨纳(Gaurav Asthan)受够了让你的家庭或工作场所变得更智能的想法，这种想法通常意味着增加更多的东西：办公桌上的智能扬声器，不断告诉你步数的智能手表，将广告插入到你早上日常生活中的智能冰箱。不仅如此，这些设备和应用程序还在不断地将你从你想要做的事情上拉出来，无论那是工作还是试图不工作。
他们想要(他们告诉我)类似于“指环王”中的魔法剑刺的东西：它99%的时间只是一把剑，但如果你需要它，它也是兽人雷达，即使你需要它，它也会发光。为什么数字世界不同样地只在你需要的时候出现，而且是以尽可能不引人注目的方式出现呢？
德沃曼之前从事高端家具设计，与阿萨纳一起提出了通过“一块木板而不是应用程序”与科技互动的想法，正如后者所说的那样。
“我们创造的是一个模块化的技术平台，它使用具有电容式触摸传感的高强度LED。这使得我们可以将其嵌入到基本上不透明的材料中，“德沃曼解释说。“墙壁、台面、书桌，在家里、办公室、零售、交通，我们看到这么多提供信息的方式和完全看不见的控制。”
当显示器关闭时，表面看起来完全正常，而且确实是这样。梅实验室在CES上展示了它自己的天然材料展示，它需要一个特别穿孔的木质表面，你可能不想把咖啡洒在上面。TouchWood显示器只是一种：木材-或许多其他常见的表面材料。
它不是第二次显示，而是通过我们的台式机、笔记本电脑和手机…呈现给我们的信息雪崩的友好溢出。还有扬声器、手表、咖啡机、机器狗等等。
阿萨纳说：“我们不是想把一台电脑放在表面上，我们想为你现有的设备提供一个更好的接触点，通过消除一些施加在它们身上的信息压力来增强它们的能力。”
也许你和我一样，经常把眼睛扫到浏览器的标签上，或者屏幕底部排列的应用程序上，看看有没有什么变化--一封新的电子邮件，一条关于Slake的消息，一个日历项目。Touchwood办公桌可以让这些通知采取不同的路线，就像你放咖啡或鼠标的地方画了一个发光的圆圈。点击那里就可以获得摘要，或者转到内容，或者把它刷走-但你永远不需要切换标签，或者转到不同的应用程序，或者解锁你的手机。当它完成的时候，桌子又变成了一块木头。
德沃曼认为这种过渡是自然的。“我们所了解的触摸屏真正出现的时间只有10年或11年。但因为它们无处不在，所以我们对它们习以为常。“他说。“当你看科幻片的时候，这项技术在未来500年里还在使用！但不应该是这样的。就汽车而言，现在的iPhone就像T型车。“。
Touchwood的目标是最终成为一个平台，但首先需要推出自己的产品。该公司计划在明年推出一张漂亮的坐/站办公桌，并提供两个大型展示区，价格在2000美元左右。是的，很贵-但你可能会惊讶于人们会很高兴地花在新家具上，特别是像一个新的重要家庭办公室的主要组成部分。
在用旗舰产品证明了这个概念之后，他们可以开始进入其他利基市场，并与合作伙伴合作。将看不见的显示器嵌入到台面、墙壁，当然也包括餐厅的桌子，这会导致各种用例。这里希望Touchwood的技术能带来一个屏幕略有减少的未来-至少我们可以看到这样的屏幕。
note_en:
Co-founders  Matthew Dworman   and  Gaurav Asthana   were fed up with the idea that making your home or workplace smarter usually meant adding even more  stuff: a smart speaker that sits on your desk, a smart watch constantly telling you your step count, a smart fridge that slips advertisements into your morning routine. Not only that but these devices and apps are constantly drawing you away from what you want to do, whether that’s work or trying  not to work.
 They wanted (they told me) something like the enchanted sword Sting from Lord of the Rings: It’s just a sword 99 percent of the time, but it’s also an orc radar if and when you need it, and even then it just glows. Why doesn’t the digital world similarly only appear when you need it, and in the least obtrusive fashion possible?
  Dworman previously worked in high-end furniture design, and with Asthana developed the idea of interacting with tech via “a slab of wood instead of an app,” as the latter put it.
  “What we’ve created is a modular tech platform that uses high-intensity LEDs with capacitive touch sensing. This allows us to embed it in essentially opaque material,” Dworman explained. “The wall, countertop, desk, in the home, the office, retail, transportation, we see so many ways to provide information and completely invisible controls.”
 The surface would appear completely normal when the display is off, and indeed it is.  Mui Labs, which demonstrated at CES its own natural material display, requires a specially perforated wood surface that you probably wouldn’t want to spill coffee on. A TouchWood display is just that: wood — or many other common surface materials.
  It’s not meant to be a second display, but a friendly overflow for the information avalanche presented to us via our desktops, laptops, and phones… and speakers, watches, coffee makers, robot dogs, and so on.
 “We’re not trying to put a computer in a surface — we want to provide you with a better touchpoint for your existing devices, to enhance their capabilities by taking away some of the information pressure that’s put on them,” said Asthana.
  Perhaps you, like me, constantly flick your eyes towards the tabs in your browsers, or the apps arrayed on the bottom of your screen, to see if there’s any change — a new email, a message on Slack, a calendar item. A TouchWood desk would let those notifications take alternative routes, like a glowing circle off where you put your coffee or mouse. Tap it there and get a summary, or go to the content, or swipe it away — but you never have to switch tabs, or go to a different app, or unlock your phone. And when it’s done, the desk is just a piece of wood again.
 Dworman sees the transition as natural. “Touchscreens the way we know them have really only been around for 10 or 11 years. But because they’re so ubiquitous we kind of take them for granted,” he said. “When you watch sci-fi films, this tech is still being used 500 years in the future! But it shouldn’t be. In car terms, the iPhone as it is now is like the Model T.”
 TouchWood aims to be a platform eventually, but needs to launch a product of its own first. It plans to have a nice sit/stand desk with two large display areas available next year for somewhere in the $2,000 region. Expensive, yes — but you may be surprised what people will happily spend on new furniture, especially something like a major component of a newly important home office.
 After proving out the concept with a flagship product, they can start working their way into other niches and working with partners. Embedding an invisible display in a countertop, wall, or of course a restaurant table leads to all kinds of use cases. Here’s hoping TouchWood’s tech leads to a future with slightly fewer screens in it — at least ones we can see.
title: Touchwood在您的办公桌、桌子和墙壁内放置了多功能的、不显眼的界面
title_en: TouchWood puts versatile, unobtrusive interfaces inside your desk, table and walls

Accel的聊天将会很有趣，原因有几个，其中之一是Braccia是大声的反面-TechCrunch在之前的报道中指出，他通常对公众评论保持沉默。但布拉西亚很早就投资了Slake，这意味着他将对直接上市市场、IPO市场、SaaS和远程工作热潮有很好的前景。我们一定会拿到最新的。
德莱克在公共领域更为活跃，曾牵头收购森德(Sennder)(最近与优步(Uber)达成交易)、Shift Technology和Avito等公司，后者去年以超过10亿美元的价格出售给Naspers。正如你可以从这一系列交易中看出的那样，德莱克将能够让我们深入了解欧洲初创企业的情况。
由于德莱克在加入风投之前是一名投资银行家，我们将拭目以待她对今天的并购和首次公开募股(IPO)环境有何看法。
总而言之，这将是我期待着主办的一次美好时光。对于额外的Crunch用户，登录详细信息如下，如果您需要访问，可以在这里获得一个便宜的试用。
在此之前，请欣赏“干扰”节目，我们周二见。别忘了带上你最好的问题，我们可能会问到其中的一个！
note_en:
If you are just catching up to Extra Crunch Live, we’ve been hosting live discussions since the early COVID-19 days here in the United States with folks  like Mark Cuban,  Plaid founder Zach Perret and  Sequoia’s Roelof Botha taking part.
 The  Accel   chat is going to be interesting for a few reasons, one of which is that Braccia is the opposite of loud — TechCrunch has  noted his general reticence to public comment in prior reporting. But Braccia was early money into Slack, which means he’ll have good perspective into the direct listing market, the IPO market writ large, SaaS and the remote-work boom. We’ll make sure to get the latest.
 De Rycker is a  bit more active in the public sphere and has lead deals into companies like  Sennder (which recently  did a deal with Uber),  Shift Technology and  Avito, which  sold to Naspers for north of $1 billion last year. As you can tell from that string of deals, De Rycker will be able to give us a working dig into what’s up in the European startup scene.
 And as De Rycker worked as an investment banker before VC, we’ll see what she has to say regarding today’s M&A and IPO climes.
 All in all, it’s going to be a good time that I am looking forward to hosting. Login details follow for Extra Crunch folks, and you can  snag a cheap trial here if you need access.
 Until then, enjoy Disrupt and we’ll see you on Tuesday. Don’t forget to bring your best questions, and we might get to one of them!

note:
虽然在这场大流行的垃圾箱大火中能够访问这些大型群组视频聊天平台是件非常棒的事情，但它们都有点…。我已经精疲力尽了。部分原因是与屏幕交谈固有的怪诞，但很大一部分原因是视频聚会不像现实世界中的同行那样流畅。当你把人们聚集在一起参加一个现实生活中的聚会时，你通常不会把30个人塞进一个大团体，集体的焦点默默地转移到任何一张设法说出一句话的脸上。你们分成几个较小的小组。每个人都有他们自己的小对话，在去食物/酒吧/浴室/任何地方的路上，转移到他们听到的任何聊天。
本周参加TechCrunch Disrupt Startup Battlefield竞争的Rally公司正在打造一个视频聊天平台，就是考虑到这一点。
Rally在“桌子”周围建立团体视频通话--想想分组会议室，除了你几乎听不到团体之间的闲聊。虽然不会让人分心，但如果另一张桌子开始谈论你喜欢的节目的最新一集，可能会吸引你的注意力。你可以点击一下跳到另一张桌子上，然后以同样快的速度跳回你的最后一次对话。
桌子放在“房间”里，每个房间一次最多可容纳35名用户。较大的活动可以有多个房间，允许像现场音乐活动这样的事情，每个房间播放不同的流派。
整个方法的目的是给平台一种近乎物理的感觉，而不需要比每个人都已经习惯的世界的缩放和相遇复杂得多的东西。它没有3D效果，也没有虚拟动画头像需要学习控制。
需要防止别人窃听吗？例如，也许你是在一个虚拟的酒吧琐事之夜，不想让其他团队无意中听到你的答案。隐私切换可以让您暂时锁定您的餐桌对话，并在时机成熟时迅速拆除墙壁。如果背景嗡嗡声太让人分心，你也可以调整其他桌子的音量。
除了能够配置东西和引导麻烦制造者离开活动之外，主持人还可以获得一些客人没有的额外功能。他们可以“上台”(单独或与他们选择的嘉宾一起)以最大音量向每个人宣布消息，或者他们可以选择随机洗桌子，以阻止人们一直与同一名参加活动的人在一起。
Rally联合创始人阿里·吉瓦尼(Ali Jiwani)告诉我，他们最初的关注点有点具体：他们正在建立一个举办现场喜剧活动的平台，这在很大程度上取决于人群的能量。当用户开始要求将该平台用于其他用途时，他们会将其重新调整为更广泛的使用案例。仍然欢迎人们来(并这样做！)。在平台上主持现场喜剧-这不再是主要的关注点。
Rally是为在浏览器内工作而构建的，无需任何下载，但这里的“浏览器”目前指的是Chrome。它可能会在其他浏览器上运行，但Rally强烈建议暂时坚持使用Chrome。
note_en:
While it’s beyond wonderful to have access to these big group video chat platforms during this pandemic dumpster fire, they’re all a bit… exhausting. Part of that is just the inherent weirdness of talking with a screen, but a big part of it is that video gatherings just don’t flow the way their real world counterparts do. When you get people together for a real-life party, you’re usually not cramming 30 people into one big group, the collective focus silently shifting to whichever face managed to get a word out. You break off into smaller groups. Everyone holds their own little conversations, moving to whichever chat catches their ear on the way to the food/bar/bathroom/whatever.
 Rally, a company competing in the TechCrunch Disrupt Startup Battlefield this week, is building a video chat platform with this in mind.
   Rally builds group video calls around “tables” – think breakout rooms, except that you can juuuust barely hear the chatter from group to group. Not so much that it’s distracting, but enough that if another table starts talking about the latest episode of a show you love, it might grab your attention. You can hop to another table with a click, then jump right back into your last conversation just as quickly.
 Tables live within “rooms,” each of which supports up to 35 users at a time. Larger events can have multiple rooms, allowing for things like live music events where each room plays a different genre.
 The whole approach is meant to give the platform an almost physical feel, without requiring much more complexity than the Zooms and Meets of the world that everyone has already grown used to. There’s no 3D aspect to it, nor virtual animated avatars to learn to control.
  Need to keep others from eavesdropping? Maybe you’re at a virtual pub trivia night, for example, and don’t want other teams overhearing your answers. A privacy toggle lets you lock down your tables conversation temporarily, and quickly take the walls back down when the time comes. You can also adjust the volume of the other tables if the background buzz gets too distracting.
 Beyond being able to configure things and boot troublemakers from the event, hosts get a few bonus features that guests don’t. They can “take the stage” (alone or with a guest of their choosing) to make announcements to everyone at full volume, or they can opt to randomly shuffle the tables to discourage people from hanging with the same fellow event goers the whole time.
 Rally co-founder Ali Jiwani tells me that their initial focus was a bit specific: They were building a platform for hosting live comedy events, so much of which depends on the energy of the crowd. When users started asking to use the platform for other things, they retuned it for broader use cases. People are still welcome to (and do!) host live comedy on the platform — it’s just not the primary focus anymore.
 Rally is built to work within the browser without any downloads, with the catch that “browser” here currently means Chrome. It might work in other browsers, but Rally strongly suggests sticking with Chrome for now.
title: 拉力赛希望让大型群组视频通话更有趣、更真实，或许也不会让人筋疲力尽
title_en: Rally wants to make big group video calls more fun, more real and maybe less exhausting

note:
这一更新被称为“巴黎”版本，也标志着自SAP前首席执行官比尔·麦克德莫特(Bill McDermott)去年11月成为总裁兼首席执行官以来，该公司首批重大发布之一。
麦克德莫特说：“我们从事的业务是有目的的运营。”“这个目的就是让职场更好地为人们服务。坦率地说，这一切都是关于人的。这就是全世界的首席执行官们都在谈论的。这种COVID环境把重点放在了人身上。在当今世界，如何让员工实现整个企业的使命？[…]。企业正在改变运营方式，以提高客户忠诚度和员工参与度。“。
他认为，在这一点上，“技术不再支持业务，技术就是业务”，但与此同时，大多数公司还没有准备好迎接任何数字颠覆。当然，ServiceNow希望将自己定位为能够帮助这些企业的平台。
“我们在ServiceNow非常幸运，”ServiceNow的首席产品官CJ Desai说。“我们是数字化转型的关键平台，因为我们的客户正在考虑转型他们的公司。”
就实际产品更新而言，ServiceNow总共推出了6款新产品。其中包括具有自动化业务影响分析的新业务连续性管理功能和连续性计划开发工具，以及面向IT团队的新硬件资产管理和面向法律运营团队的法律服务交付。
通过为金融服务和电信用户提供专门的解决方案，该公司现在还将其现有的一些解决方案与为这些客户提供的更专业的服务结合在一起。正如ServiceNow的戴夫·赖特(Dave Wright)所指出的那样，这远远超出了将现有模块组合在一起的范围。
“第一个要素实际上是熟悉业务，”他解释说。“因此，实际制造产品的技术并不那么难。这是相对较快的。但当您查看所有这些工作流时，其独特性在于运营与客户服务端的连接。电信公司就是一个很好的例子。你已经接通了电信网络运营方，确保所有的运营方设备都处于活动状态。然后是客户服务管理方面的业务服务，看看客户是如何获得服务的。现在，有趣的是，因为我们把这两件事都放在一个平台上，我们可以非常容易地将它们联系在一起。“。
赖特指出，在机器学习方面，ServiceNow在过去四年中在该领域进行了六次收购-现在开始收到回报。具体地说，该公司将在此次发布中推出新的预测智能工作台。这项新服务使流程所有者可以更容易地检测问题，同时还可以向代理建议相关任务和内容，并自动确定传入请求的优先级。使用无监督学习，系统还可以识别其他类型的模式，并且通过一些预先构建的模板，用户也可以构建自己的解决方案。
德赛说：“ServiceNow的优势一直是一个架构、一个数据模型和一个诞生在云中的平台，提供公司需要的工作流以及员工和客户期望的出色体验。”Now Platform Paris发布提供了由人工智能支持的智能体验、弹性运营和优化支出的能力。它们将共同为企业提供所需的敏捷性，帮助它们在COVID经济中蓬勃发展。“
note_en:
Dubbed the “ Paris”   release, this update also marks one of the first major releases for the company since former SAP CEO Bill McDermott became its president and CEO last November.
 “We are in the business of operating on purpose,” McDermott said. “And that purpose is to make the world of work work better for people. And frankly, it’s all about people. That’s all CEOs talk about all around the world. This COVID environment has put the focus on people. In today’s world, how do you get people to achieve missions across the enterprise? […] Businesses are changing how they run to drive customer loyalty and employee engagement.”
   He argues that at this point, “technology is no longer supporting the business, technology is the business,” but at the same time, the majority of companies aren’t prepared to meet whatever digital disruption comes their way.  ServiceNow,   of course, wants to position itself as the platform that can help these businesses.
 “We are very fortunate at  ServiceNow,”   CJ Desai, ServiceNow’s chief product officer, said. “We are the critical platform for digital transformation, as our customers are thinking about transforming their companies.”
 As far as the actual product updates, ServiceNow is launching a total of six new products. These include new business continuity management features with automated business impact analysis and tools for continuity plan development, as well as new hardware asset management for IT teams and legal service delivery for legal operations teams.
  With specialized solutions for financial services and telco users, the company is also now bringing together some of its existing solutions with more specialized services for these customers. As ServiceNow’s Dave Wright noted, this goes well beyond just putting together existing blocks.
 “The first element is actually getting familiar with the business,” he explained. “So the technology, actually building the product, isn’t that hard. That’s relatively quick. But the uniqueness when you look at all of these workflows, it’s the connection of the operations to the customer service side. Telco is a great example. You’ve got the telco network operations side, making sure that all the operational equipment is active. And then you’ve got the business service side with customer service management, looking at how the customers are getting service. Now, the interesting thing is, because we’ve got both things sitting on one platform, we can link those together really easily.”
  On the machine learning side, ServiceNow made six acquisitions in the area in the last four years, Wright noted — and that is now starting to pay off. Specifically, the company is launching its new predictive intelligence workbench with this release. This new service makes it easier for process owners to detect issues, while also suggesting relevant tasks and content to agents, for example, and prioritizing incoming requests automatically. Using unsupervised learning, the system can also identify other kinds of patterns and with a number of pre-built templates, users can build their own solutions, too.
 “The ServiceNow advantage has always been one architecture, one data model and one born-in-the-cloud platform that delivers workflows companies need and great experiences employees and customers expect,” said Desai. “The Now Platform Paris release provides smart experiences powered by AI, resilient operations, and the ability to optimize spend. Together, they will provide businesses with the agility they need to help them thrive in the COVID economy.”
title: ServiceNow更新其工作流自动化平台

note:
进入Luther.AI，它号称是谷歌，通过捕获和转录音频记录，同时使用AI在另一次在线对话的时刻或通过搜索从你的虚拟内存库传递正确的信息。
该公司本周将在TechCrunch Disrupt大会上发布其产品的首个基于浏览器的版本，该公司将在TechCrunch Disrupt Battlefield上争夺10万美元的奖金。
人工智能的创始人说，该公司是建立在人类记忆容易出错的前提下的，而这种弱点限制了我们的个人智力。Luther.AI背后的想法是提供一种工具来保持、回忆甚至增强我们自己的大脑。
这是一个很高的要求，但该公司的创始人相信，通过人工智能和其他技术日益增长的力量，这是可能的。
“通过神经科学、NLP和区块链的融合，它能够提供无缝的即时召回。GPT-3建立在公共互联网的记忆之上，而路德建立在你的私人自我记忆之上，“公司创始人兼首席执行官Suman Kanuganti告诉TechCrunch。
它从记录你全天的互动开始。首先，这将是浏览器中的在线会议，因为我们发现自己所处的时代，这是我们最经常互动的方式。不过，随着时间的推移，他们设想的是一种高质量的5G记录设备，你可以全天佩戴在工作中，并捕捉你的互动。
如果从隐私的角度来看，这会让您感到担忧，那么从高端加密开始，Luther正在构建一些保护措施。此外，您只能在具有其他方的显式许可的情况下保存他们的对话部分。“从技术上讲，我们让用户成为他们所说内容的所有者。因此，例如，如果你和我在现实世界中进行对话，除非你提供明确的许可，否则你的记忆不会从这次与我的特别对话中分享，“卡努甘蒂解释说。
最后，每个人都拥有自己在路德的数据，其他任何人都不能从路德或任何其他个人访问或使用这些对话。他们最终将使用区块链技术强制实施这一所有权，尽管卡努甘蒂表示，这将被添加到该产品的未来版本中。
卡努甘蒂说，该产品的真正威力不会在公司内部几个人使用该产品的情况下实现，而是在数十人或数百人使用的网络效应中实现，尽管它对个人帮助回忆记忆也有实用价值，他说。
虽然他们本周将发布这款基于浏览器的产品，但他们最终将拥有一款独立的应用程序，还可以设想未来其他应用程序可以通过API利用这项技术，开发人员可以在其他应用程序中内置路德功能。
该公司是由卡努甘蒂和包括首席技术官莎伦·张、设计总监克里斯蒂·凯泽和科学家马克·埃特林格在内的三名联合创始人于今年年初创立的。它已经筹集了50万美元，目前包括创始人在内有14名员工。
note_en:
Enter  Luther.AI, which purports to be Google for your memory by capturing and transcribing audio recordings, while using AI to deliver the right information from your virtual memory bank in the moment of another online conversation or via search.
 The company is releasing an initial browser-based version of their product this week at TechCrunch Disrupt where it’s competing for the $100,000 prize at TechCrunch Disrupt Battlefield.
  Luther.AI’s founders say the company is built on the premise that human memory is fallible, and that weakness limits our individual intelligence. The idea behind Luther.AI is to provide a tool to retain, recall and even augment our own brains.
 It’s a tall order, but the company’s founders believe it’s possible through the growing power of artificial intelligence and other technologies.
 “It’s made possible through a convergence of neuroscience, NLP and blockchain to deliver seamless in-the-moment recall.  GPT-3 is built on the memories of the public internet, while Luther is built on the memories of your private self,” company founder and CEO Suman Kanuganti told TechCrunch.
 It starts by recording your interactions throughout the day. For starters, that will be online meetings in a browser, as we find ourselves in a time where that is the way we interact most often. Over time though, they envision a high-quality 5G recording device you wear throughout your day at work and capture your interactions.
 If that is worrisome to you from a privacy perspective, Luther is building in a few safeguards starting with high-end encryption. Further, you can only save other parties’ parts of a conversation with their explicit permission. “Technologically, we make users the owner of what they are speaking. So for example, if you and I are having a conversation in the physical world unless you provide explicit permission, your memories are not shared from this particular conversation with me,” Kanuganti explained.
 Finally, each person owns their own data in Luther and nobody else can access or use these conversations either from Luther or any other individual. They will eventually enforce this ownership using blockchain technology, although Kanuganti says that will be added in a future version of the product.
  Kanuganti says the true power of the product won’t be realized with a few individuals using the product inside a company, but in the network effect of having dozens or hundreds of people using it, even though it will have utility even for an individual to help with memory recall, he said.
 While they are releasing the browser-based product this week, they will eventually have a stand-alone app, and can also envision other applications taking advantage of the technology in the future via an API where developers can build Luther functionality into other apps.
 The company was founded at the beginning of this year by Kanuganti and three co-founders including CTO  Sharon Zhang,   design director Kristie Kaiser and scientist  Marc Ettlinger  . It has raised $500,000 and currently has 14 employees including the founders.
title: 人工智能Luther.AI是一款新的人工智能工具，在个人对话中的作用类似于谷歌
title_en: Luther.AI is a new AI tool that acts like Google for personal conversations

note:
教育技术公司正在寻找进入蓬勃发展的市场的新方法。正如Course Hero联合创始人安德鲁·格雷尔(Andrew Graeur)所说，由于新冠肺炎疫情的爆发，他的问答平台的目标从拥有100万订户变成了“数百万”。

这家初创公司的一位发言人表示，这轮融资没有改变其估值。这家初创公司在5月份成为估值超过14亿美元的独角兽公司。取而代之的是，融资“与其说是关于资金，不如说是关于我们因此能够引入的合作伙伴。”
note_en:
Edtech companies are searching for new ways to tap into a booming market. As  Course Hero co-founder Andrew Graeur  put it, the goal for his Q&A platform went from reaching a million subscribers to “many millions” as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic.
 One way edtech companies are approaching these unprecedented times is by raising extension rounds that are earmarked specifically to bring on strategic partners from around the world. The approach of trading equity for a chance at globalization is neither rare nor cheap, but comes with new weight given the sector’s boom.
 Today,  ApplyBoard   closed a $55 million extension round for its Series C, which now totals $130 million. ApplyBoard helps international students search and apply to universities and colleges across the world. It wants users to think of it as a  Common App for international students, serving as a college undergrad application.
 A spokesperson for the startup — which became a unicorn valued at over  $1.4 billion in May — says the round did not change its valuation. Instead, the financing was “less about funding, and more about the partners that we were able to bring on board as a result.”
title: 理解3轮EDTECH扩展回合的意义
title_en: Making sense of 3 edtech extension rounds
